title: Administration Analysis
subtitle: Deep dive into procurement patterns across Mexican presidential administrations (2002–2025)
groundTruthNote: Known corruption cases documented in this period are tracked in the Ground Truth section, including vendor matches, contract counts, and detection rates for each case.
documentedCasesNote: Manually verified from public records and ASF investigations — not ML-detected
noData: No yearly data available for this administration
noEvents: No events recorded for this period
incompleteDatasetDescription: Sheinbaum's administration began October 2024. This data covers ~4 months vs. 6-year full terms for other presidents. Direct comparisons may be misleading.
sectorCorrelationsNote: Sector pairs with correlated risk trajectories (|r| ≥ 0.70) during this administration
heatmapSubtitle: Risk metrics by sector for the selected administration — sorted by high-risk contract percentage.
transitionSubtitle: Changes in key indicators between consecutive administrations. Values marked ! or !! are statistically unusual (≥1.8σ from historical average).
keyEventsSubtitle: Legislative reforms, documented scandals, and crises that affected public procurement patterns during this administration.
comparisonTableDesc: Average metrics across five administrations — based on actual COMPRANET data. Selected administration highlighted in blue.
matrixTitle: Risk Matrix by Administration and Sector
matrixSubtitle: Volume-weighted averages — toggle metric with buttons on the right

fingerprints.title: Presidential Procurement Fingerprints
fingerprints.subtitle: How each administration's procurement patterns compare across risk dimensions
